features.name: Contract system
features.description: Zod-validated Workflow/Item/Action contract that drives all generic UI rendering. Single source of truth — workflows are data, not code.

features.name: Contract-driven dashboard, queue & flyout
features.description: Generic shell renders any workflow's queue (table/cards), detail flyout (source vs proposed panes), bulk actions and hotkeys purely from the contract.

features.name: Authentication & route guard
features.description: Better Auth email/password session plus cookie-based middleware guard redirecting unauthenticated users.

features.name: Database schema & unified seed
features.description: Drizzle schema for workflows + workflow_items alongside Better Auth tables, with one-command idempotent seed (admin user + domain tables + workflows/items).
features.evidence: db/schema.ts, db/index.ts, scripts/seed.ts; run `npx tsx scripts/seed.ts` (needs DATABASE_URL)

features.name: Inbound webhook ingestion
features.description: POST /api/workflows/[id]/items endpoint: HMAC-authenticated, ItemSchema-validated, idempotent on item.id; external engines enqueue and settle items.

features.name: Outbound decision dispatch
features.description: Reviewer decisions persist immediately as `dispatching`, then fire an HMAC-signed outbound POST to the action handler URL, validated against a per-workflow SSRF allowlist.

features.name: Green the baseline lint gate
features.description: `./init.sh` currently fails on 3 pre-existing eslint errors: react-hooks/set-state-in-effect in app/dashboard/DashboardClient.tsx:219 and app/data/DataClient.tsx:144, and react/jsx-no-comment-textnodes in app/data/DataClient.tsx:187. `npx tsc --noEmit` is clean. Resolve these (the set-state-in-effect effects intentionally re-sync on prop change — verify behavior is preserved) so the baseline gate passes.
